# Get Started with Maps SDK for iOS | Maps SDK | iOS | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This guide will walk you through installing the most recent version of the **Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS**, configuring your iOS app to use the Maps SDK, and initializing a Mapbox map in your iOS app.

Prerequisites[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#prerequisites "Prerequisites への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **A Mapbox account**: Sign up or log into a free account on [Mapbox](https://account.mapbox.com/auth/signup/)
    .
*   **Xcode**: Install Xcode to your device through the Macbook App Store.

Part 1: Configure your credentials[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#part-1-create-and-configure-your-credentials "Part 1: Configure your credentials への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before starting to develop your application with the Maps SDK, you'll need to create and configure your credentials.

### Step 1: Log in/Sign up for a Mapbox account[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#step-1-login-sign-up "Step 1: Log in/Sign up for a Mapbox account への直接リンク")

Login to your [Mapbox account](https://console.mapbox.com/)
. If you don't have an account, you can [sign up for free](https://account.mapbox.com/auth/signup/)
.

Your account includes a default public access token and allows you to create a secret access token for use in the installation of the SDK.

### Step 2: Configure your public token[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#step-2-configure-your-public-token "Step 2: Configure your public token への直接リンク")

Follow these steps to grab a public access token from your Mapbox account and add it to your project's `Info.plist`.

1.  Open your project or create a new project in Xcode.
    *   If creating a new project, we recommend using the `App` project type.
2.  Navigate to your project's `Info.plist` file.
    *   This is located in your main project page. You can go here by double clicking the project title in the top left corner, then `Info` in the middle of the screen.
3.  Hover over a key and click the plus button.
4.  Type `MBXAccessToken` into the key field and hit tab to select the value field.
5.  Grab your default public access token.
    *   To grab your token, open up a browser and go to your account's [tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
        .
    *   On the top of your token list, copy your **Default Public Token**.
6.  Go back to Xcode and in the value field, paste in your default public token.

Your public access token is now available for use in your iOS project and you will be able to access the Mapbox SDK.

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/assets/medias/MapsInstallPublicToken-eb2f6fbf8715def5ae8eaa35a5b562c4.mp4)
.

**Advanced Topics: Best Practices, Rotating Tokens & Adding Tokens at Runtime**

[GUIDE\
\
Access token best practices\
\
Learn how to keep access tokens private in mobile apps.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/private-access-token-android-and-ios/)

**Adding Tokens at Runtime**

You can also implement tokens at runtime, but this requires you to have a separate server to store your tokens. This is helpful if you want to rotate your tokens or add additional security by storing your tokens outside of the application, but is a much more complex method of implementation.

If you do choose to follow this method, we recommend calling `MapboxOptions.accessToken = YOUR_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN` before inflating the `MapView`, to avoid errors from occurring.

**Rotating Tokens**

For more information on access token rotation, consult the [Access Tokens Information page](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/how-mapbox-works/access-tokens/)
.

Part 2: Add the dependency[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#part-2-add-the-dependency "Part 2: Add the dependency への直接リンク")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mapbox provides the Maps SDK via **Swift Package Manager**, **CocoaPods** and **direct download**. You can choose whichever you prefer.

Swift Package Manager

CocoaPods

Direct download

Follow the steps below to download and install the Mapbox Maps SDK via the Swift Package Manager.

1.  In your Xcode project or workspace, go to **File** > **Add Packages Dependencies...**.
2.  Enter the following GitHub URL into the search bar in the top right corner:

    https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-ios.git

3.  Select the _Dependency Rule_ you want to apply to the Maps SDK
    *   We recommend selecting "Up to Next Major Version" for the _Dependency Rule_, specifying `11.0.0` as the minimum version. This allows you to select the most recently released stable minor version of the SDK.
    *   To instead install a specific version set the _Dependency Rule_ field to "Exact Version" and insert the desired version. The latest stable version of the Maps SDK is `11.13.2`.
4.  Hit enter or Click **Add Package**.
5.  In the new window, click the dropdown under `Add to Target` and select your project target.
6.  Click **Add Package**.
7.  Once the SDK finishes installing, make sure you can see 4 new dependencies on the left side of the editor, under **Package Dependencies**:
    *   `MapboxCommon`
    *   `MapboxCoreMaps`
    *   `MapboxMaps`
    *   `Turf`
8.  Next, click on your project's target, scroll down to `Frameworks, Libraries, and Embedded Content` and make sure `MapboxMaps` is included.
    *   To access your project's target, click the name of your project on the left side of your editor, then on the left side of the inner window, selecting the first name underneath the **Target** header.
    *   If the library is not added, click the `+` button and from the list that appears, select `MapboxMaps` and click **Add**.

Now you can use start using Mapbox in your project by adding `import MapboxMaps` to the top of any of your files.

Your browser doesn't support HTML5 video. Open [link to the video](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/assets/medias/MapsInstallSpm-7efae6e51e075fecd2c355853e1b73e7.mp4)
.

Updating your packages

If you are using the Swift Package Manager and need to update your packages select **File** > **Packages** > **Update To Latest Package Versions**.

Part 3: Add a map[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#part-3-add-a-map "Part 3: Add a map への直接リンク")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can now add a map to your application using either UIKit or SwiftUI, by following these steps:

1.  On the left side of the editor, select the folder with your project name.
2.  Click on the file name `YOURPROJECTNAMEApp`.
3.  Copy the code snippet below and add it to file.
4.  Run your application and you should see an interactable map appear in the simulator.
    *   When running the simulator, make sure to set your build platform to a type of iOS device, for example `iPhone 16`. If you see `My Mac` or `Any iOS Device` at the top of Xcode, switch the type to a specific iOS device.

SwiftUI

UIKit

SwiftUI

UIKit

    import SwiftUIimport MapboxMapsstruct ContentView: View {    var body: some View {        let center = CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: 39.5, longitude: -98.0)        Map(initialViewport: .camera(center: center, zoom: 2, bearing: 0, pitch: 0))            .ignoresSafeArea()    }}

SwiftUI

UIKit

    import UIKitimport MapboxMapsclass ViewController: UIViewController {    override func viewDidLoad() {        super.viewDidLoad()        let mapView = MapView(frame: view.bounds)        let cameraOptions = CameraOptions(center:            CLLocationCoordinate2D(latitude: 39.5, longitude: -98.0),            zoom: 2, bearing: 0, pitch: 0)        mapView.mapboxMap.setCamera(to: cameraOptions)        mapView.autoresizingMask = [.flexibleWidth, .flexibleHeight]        view.addSubview(mapView)    }}

Troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#troubleshooting "Troubleshooting への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   If the `MapboxMaps` library is not accessible from your code, try the following:
    *   Make sure the 4 libraries are listed in Frameworks, Libraries, and Embedded Content.
        *   You should see the 4 libraries: `MapboxCommon`, `MapboxCoreMaps`, `MapboxMaps`,`Turf`.
        *   If these are missing, click the plus button and add each of them from the list.
    *   Check that `Embed` dropdown for each library is assigned `Embed & Sign` in the Frameworks, Libraries, and Embedded Content section.
    *   Remove and re-add package if the information above is true.
*   If you cannot run the map in your simulator, try the following:
    *   Make sure your token is added to the Info.plist and is a valid token.
    *   Check that build platform is assigned to a specific iOS device, such as `iPhone 16`. If you see `My Mac` or `Any iOS Device`, this may cause issues when trying to run the simulator.

[GUIDE\
\
Troubleshooting Mapbox Maps and Navigation SDKs for iOS installation\
\
This guide provides more information for troubleshooting installation problems with the Maps SDK.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/)

Next Steps[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#next-steps "Next Steps への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

With the Maps SDK installed and a minimal map rendering in your app, you can explore additional features of the SDK:

*   [Access the user's location](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/user-location/)
    
*   [Understand Markers and Map Annotations](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/markers-and-annotations/)
    
*   [Learn more about the camera and map animations](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/camera-and-animation/)
    

You can also explore example code and tutorials:

[TUTORIAL\
\
Learn how to clone and run the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS examples app.\
\
Learn how to clone and run the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS examples app to see any iOS example in real time.](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/maps-sdk-ios-examples-app/)
[EXAMPLE\
\
Add a marker to the map using Point Annotations\
\
Learn how to add a marker to a point of interest on a map by using point annotations on a symbol layer.](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/examples/point-annotation/)
[EXAMPLE\
\
Add a pop-up to the map using View Annotations\
\
Learn how to add a pop-up to a point of interest on a map by using view annotations.](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/examples/view-annotation-basic/)

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

mapbox-maps-ios

, applies them to vector tiles that conform to the [Mapbox Vector Tile Specification](https://github.com/mapbox/vector-tile-spec)

.

# expression | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/expression/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

An **expression** defines a formula for computing the value of any [layout property, paint property](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/layout-paint-property/)
, or [filter](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/filter/)
 within a map style. Expressions allow you to style data with multiple feature properties at once, apply conditional logic, and manipulate data with mathematical, logical, and string operators. This allows for sophisticated [runtime styling](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/runtime-styling/)
.

For a complete list of all available expressions, see the [Mapbox Style Specification](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/)
.

How to use expressions in Mapbox Studio

Learn how to use expressions for string and data operations to create a map with bilingual labels.

### Expressions in Mapbox GL JS[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/expression/#expressions-in-mapbox-gl-js "Direct link to Expressions in Mapbox GL JS")

[Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-gl-js/)
 expressions uses a Lisp-like syntax, using JSON arrays. Expressions in Mapbox GL JS follow this format:

    [expression_name, argument_0, argument_1, ...]

The `expression_name` is the expression operator. For example, you would use [`*`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#*)
 to multiply two arguments:

    ['*', ['pi'], ['^', 3, 2]]

The first argument is [`pi`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#pi)
, which is an expression that returns the mathematical constant pi. The second argument is another expression in which [`^`](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/#%5E)
 has two arguments of its own. It will return 32, and the result will be multiplied by pi.

### Expressions in the mobile Maps SDKs[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/expression/#expressions-in-the-mobile-maps-sdks "Direct link to Expressions in the mobile Maps SDKs")

The [Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-ios-sdk/)
 uses [`NSExpression` and `NSPredicate` syntax](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/6.4.1/predicates-and-expressions.html)
 for expressions instead of JSON arrays.

In the [Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/mapbox-android-sdk/)
 expressions are JSON arrays, in which the first element is the expression operator and the other elements are the arguments to the expression. For more information on using expressions in the Maps SDK for Android, see the [expressions documentation page](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/styles/use-expressions/)
. Expressions are an important part of the Maps SDK's concept of _data-driven styling_. The [Mapbox Annotations Plugin for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/plugins/overview/annotation/)
 also simplifies using expressions to adjust the visual properties of annotations on a Mapbox map.

* * *

**Related resources:**

*   [Expression documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/style-spec/reference/expressions/)
    
*   Tutorial: [Get started with Mapbox GL JS expressions](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/tutorials/mapbox-gl-js-expressions/)
    
*   [Maps SDK for Android: Expressions documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/guides/styles/use-expressions/)
    
*   [Maps SDK for iOS: Expressions documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/styles/use-expressions/)
    
*   Tutorial: [Create interactive hover effects with Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/tutorials/create-interactive-hover-effects-with-mapbox-gl-js/)

# SwiftUI - Create geofence zone around user's location | Maps SDK | iOS | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/examples/swiftui-geofencing/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This example shows the usage of the **Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS** [Geofencing API](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/11.13.2/documentation/mapboxcommon/geofencingservice/)
 to create a geofence zone for a radius around the user's location, updating its color based on [events](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/11.13.2/documentation/mapboxcommon/geofencingevent/)
 such as entering, dwelling, or leaving the geofence zone.

iOS Examples App Available

This example code is part of the **Maps SDK for iOS Examples App**, a working iOS project [available on Github](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-ios/tree/v11.13.2/Sources/Examples)
. iOS developers are encouraged to run the examples app locally to interact with this example in an emulator and explore other features of the Maps SDK.

See our [Run the Maps SDK for iOS Examples App](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/tutorials/maps-sdk-ios-examples-app/)
 tutorial for step-by-step instructions.

Swift

GeofencingUserLocation.swift

[github View on GitHub](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-maps-ios/blob/main/Sources/Examples/SwiftUI%20Examples/GeofencingUserLocation.swift "View GeofencingUserLocation.swift on GitHub")

    import Foundationimport SwiftUI@_spi(Experimental) import MapboxMaps@_spi(Experimental) import MapboxCommon/// This is an Example for Experimental API that is subject to change.struct GeofencingUserLocation: View {    @State private var initialLocation: CLLocationCoordinate2D?    @ObservedObject private var geofencing = Geofencing()    private let initialLocationProvider = InitialLocationProvider()    var body: some View {        MapReader { proxy in            Map(initialViewport: .followPuck(zoom: 16)) {                Puck2D(bearing: .heading)                if let initialLocation = initialLocation {                    GeofenceCircle(id: "circle", location: initialLocation, event: geofencing.lastEvent)                }            }            .onMapLoaded { _ in startGeofencing(proxy.location) }        }        .ignoresSafeArea()    }    func startGeofencing(_ locationManager: LocationManager?) {        geofencing.start {            initialLocationProvider.start(locationManager: locationManager) { initialLocation in                var feature = Feature(geometry: .geofenceCircle(initialLocation))                feature.identifier = .string("geofence-source-circle")                feature = feature.properties([GeofencingPropertiesKeys.dwellTimeKey: 1])                    geofencing.add(feature: feature, onSuccess: { _initialLocation.wrappedValue = initialLocation })            }        }    }}private struct GeofenceCircle: MapStyleContent {    var id: String    var location: CLLocationCoordinate2D    var event: GeofenceEvent?    var body: some MapStyleContent {        GeoJSONSource(id: "geofence-source-\(id)")            .data(.geofenceCircle(location))        FillLayer(id: "geofence-layer-\(id)", source: "geofence-source-\(id)")            .fillColor(color(for: event))            .fillOpacity(0.5)    }    private func color(for event: GeofenceEvent?) -> UIColor {        switch event?.type {        case .none:            return .yellow        case .entry:            return .blue        case .exit:            return .red        case .dwell:            return .green        }    }}private final class InitialLocationProvider {    private var cancellables = Set<AnyCancelable>()    func start(locationManager: LocationManager?, _ onIntialLocation: @escaping (CLLocationCoordinate2D) -> Void) {        locationManager?.onLocationChange            .debounce(for: .seconds(0.5), scheduler: RunLoop.main)            .sink { [weak self] locations in                guard let location = locations.first else { return print("No locations received") }                onIntialLocation(location.coordinate)                self?.cancellables.removeAll()            }            .store(in: &cancellables)    }}private final class Geofencing: ObservableObject {    @Published var lastEvent: GeofenceEvent?    func start(_ completion: @escaping () -> Void) {        let geofencing = GeofencingFactory.getOrCreate()        geofencing.configure(options: GeofencingOptions()) { [weak self] result in            guard let self else { return }            /// Geofences are store in database on disk.            /// To make example isolated and synchronised with UI we try to delete existing feature from database.            geofencing.clearFeatures { result in                print("Clear features: \(result)")                geofencing.addObserver(observer: self) { result in print("Add observer: \(result)") }                completion()            }        }    }    func add(feature: Turf.Feature, onSuccess: @escaping () -> Void) {        let geofencing = GeofencingFactory.getOrCreate()        geofencing.addFeature(feature: feature) { result in            print("Add feature result: \(result)")            if case .success = result { onSuccess() }        }    }    func remove(featureId: String, completion: @escaping () -> Void) {        let geofencing = GeofencingFactory.getOrCreate()        geofencing.removeFeature(identifier: featureId) { result in            print("Remove feature result: \(result)")            completion()        }    }}extension Geofencing: GeofencingObserver {    func onEntry(event: GeofencingEvent) {        DispatchQueue.main.async { self.lastEvent = GeofenceEvent(type: .entry, feature: event.feature) }    }    func onDwell(event: GeofencingEvent) {        DispatchQueue.main.async { self.lastEvent = GeofenceEvent(type: .dwell, feature: event.feature) }    }    func onExit(event: GeofencingEvent) {        DispatchQueue.main.async { self.lastEvent = GeofenceEvent(type: .exit, feature: event.feature) }    }    func onError(error: GeofencingError) {}    func onUserConsentChanged(isConsentGiven: Bool) {}}private struct GeofenceEvent {    enum GeofenceEventType {        case entry        case dwell        case exit    }    var type: GeofenceEventType    var feature: Turf.Feature}private extension GeoJSONSourceData {    static func geofenceCircle(_ center: LocationCoordinate2D) -> GeoJSONSourceData {        .geometry(.geofenceCircle(center))    }}private extension Turf.Geometry {    static func geofenceCircle(_ center: LocationCoordinate2D) -> Turf.Geometry {        .polygon(Polygon(center: center, radius: 30, vertices: 64))    }}

このexampleは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# Navigation SDK v2 | iOS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK for iOS
======================

Current version:`v2.20.0`[View changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    Navigation UI components ready to integrate into your application
    
*   check
    
    Professionally designed map styles
    
*   check
    
    Worldwide driving, cycling, and walking directions
    
*   check
    
    Traffic avoidance and proactive rerouting
    

[githubContribute on GitHub](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios)

A newer version of the Navigation SDK is available

This page uses 2.20.0 of the Mapbox Navigation SDK. A newer version of the SDK is available. Learn about the latest version, 3.10.1, in the [Navigation SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/guides/)
.

The Navigation SDK for iOS allows you to build a complete navigation experience with the power of the [Mapbox Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/)
. You can build a complete turn-by-turn navigation UI inside your application using our pre-built components or use the core logic directly to build something truly custom.

The Navigation SDK provides a collection of features that are critical when building navigation projects, including:

*   Navigation routes
*   Accurate device location updates
*   Voice instruction announcements
*   Real-time user progress to their destination
*   Off-route detection and rerouting
*   Dynamic camera adjustments during turn-by-turn navigation
*   Pre-built UI components to show changing navigation information
*   Support for navigating with limited or no network connection

Types of navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#types-of-navigation "Direct link to Types of navigation")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK offers two types of navigation: turn-by-turn navigation and free-drive navigation.

### Turn-by-turn navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#turn-by-turn-navigation "Direct link to Turn-by-turn navigation")

Turn-by-turn navigation, also referred to as _active guidance_, is likely what comes to mind when you think about a typical navigation application: a user receives turn-by-turn instructions as they progress along the route. Read more in the [Turn-by-turn navigation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/turn-by-turn-navigation/)
 guides.

### Free-drive navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#free-drive-navigation "Direct link to Free-drive navigation")

Free-drive navigation, also referred to as _passive navigation_, is a unique Mapbox Navigation SDK feature that allows drivers to take advantage of some navigation features without selecting a route and following turn-by-turn instructions to a set destination. Read more in the [Free-drive navigation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/free-drive/)
 guide.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is compatible with applications that are:

*   Built in Xcode 13.3.1 or higher
*   Written in Swift 5.6 or higher
*   Run on iOS 12 or higher

Use with other Mapbox tools[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#use-with-other-mapbox-tools "Direct link to Use with other Mapbox tools")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK for iOS is designed to be used with several other Mapbox products.

### Directions API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#directions-api "Direct link to Directions API")

The Navigation SDK uses the Mapbox Directions API to generate routes between two or more coordinates. The Mapbox Directions for Swift framework has many route options like setting a profile identifier (for example driving, driving with traffic, or walking), waypoint heading (to influence the direction in which a route will begin), and `roadClassesToAvoid` (for example, avoiding tolls). Read more about Mapbox [Directions for Swift in the API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/directions/api/2.14.1/)
.

### Maps SDK for iOS[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#maps-sdk-for-ios "Direct link to Maps SDK for iOS")

By default, the Navigation SDK comes with access to the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS. The Navigation SDK uses the Maps SDK to display routes on a map and show the user's location on the map throughout their trip to provide both navigation-related context and nearby places that may be of interest to the driver. The Navigation SDK uses Mapbox Maps SDK v10, you can find documentation for your version in the [Maps SDK API references listing](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api-reference/)
.

Conditions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#conditions "Direct link to Conditions")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#attribution "Direct link to Attribution")

If you use a Mapbox map in your application, you are required to provide attribution according to the [Mapbox Maps SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/guides/#attribution)
.

### Terms[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#terms "Direct link to Terms")

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is distributed under [Mapbox's Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos)
.

### Pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#pricing "Direct link to Pricing")

Read more about pricing for the Navigation SDK in the [pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/pricing/)
.

Older versions of the SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/v2/guides/#older-versions-of-the-sdk "Direct link to Older versions of the SDK")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Looking for an older version of the Navigation SDK? See the [Navigation SDK v1 documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/guides/)
.

# Mapbox  Reference

[Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS](https://www.mapbox.com/ios-sdk/)

===========================================================

The Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS is an open-source framework for embedding interactive map views with scalable, customizable vector maps into Cocoa Touch applications on iOS 9.0 and above using Objective-C, Swift, or Interface Builder. It takes stylesheets that conform to the [Mapbox Style Specification](https://www.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-style-spec/)
, applies them to vector tiles that conform to the [Mapbox Vector Tile Specification](https://www.mapbox.com/developers/vector-tiles/)
, and renders them using OpenGL.

![Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS screenshots](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/6.3.0/img/screenshot.png)

For setup information, check out the [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS homepage](https://www.mapbox.com/ios-sdk/)
. For detailed usage instructions, read “[First steps with the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS](https://www.mapbox.com/help/first-steps-ios-sdk/)
” and consult the [online examples](https://www.mapbox.com/ios-sdk/examples/)
.

If you have any questions, please see [our help page](https://www.mapbox.com/help/)
. We welcome your [bug reports, feature requests, and contributions](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/issues/)
.

Changes in version 6.3.0
------------------------

This version does not support Apple Silicon Macs (arm64).

### 🐞 Bug fixes

*   Fixed issues on iOS 14 where `-[UIView layoutSubviews]` was being repeatedly called. ([#501](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/501)
    , [#507](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/507)
    )
*   Fixed a bug with UIViews being incorrectly updated with a one frame delay. ([#483](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/483)
    )
*   Fixed an issue where CocoaPods users could not install the SDK when using Xcode 12. ([#482](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/482)
    )

### 🔧 Dependencies

*   Updated `mapbox-events-ios` to `0.10.5` in order to add additional iOS 14 support.([#510](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/510)
    )
*   Core library updated to `5.2.1`. ([#537](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/537)
    )

### ✨ Other changes

*   The default branch is now `main`. Please rebase any existing branches you may have. ([#489](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/489)
    )
*   Added `MGLGlyphsRasterizationMode`, which can be configured in order to specify whether glyphs and ideographs are rendered by the server or locally. ([#517](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/pull/517)
    )

Recent changes
--------------

See the [full changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-ios/blob/ios-v6.3.0/platform/ios/CHANGELOG.md)
 for previous releases.

# MapView  Reference

[MapView\
=======](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/MapView)

*   `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapView) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapInitOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptionsProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(pl)MapInitOptionsProvider) `
    
    A protocol used to provide `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapInitOptions.html) ` when initializing a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/MapView.html) ` with a Storyboard or a nib.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Protocols/MapInitOptionsProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/ResourceOptions)

*   `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ResourceOptionsV) `
    
    Options to configure access to a resource
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Extensions/MapOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlyphsRasterizationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMGlyphsRasterizationOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Extensions/GlyphsRasterizationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[InterfaceOrientationProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28InterfaceOrientationProviderP) `
    
    A protocol that supplies current interface orientation for the specified view.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target (e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Protocols/InterfaceOrientationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionURLOpener](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps20AttributionURLOpenerP) `
    
    A protocol to open attribution URLs.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target(e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Protocols/AttributionURLOpener.html)
    
    #### Declaration

# Navigation | API Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

The **Mapbox Navigation Service** includes several APIs for getting directions or building navigation experiences, either by interacting with the Navigation Service APIs directly or using a mobile SDK. This includes services for requesting [directions](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/)
, creating [isochrones](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/isochrone/)
, [matching routes](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/map-matching/)
 to the road network on a map, and more.

Integrate the Navigation Service APIs into mobile apps with the [Mapbox Navigation SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/overview/)
 and [Mapbox Navigation SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/navigation/overview/)
.

[Directions API\
\
The Mapbox Directions API calculates optimal routes and produces turn-by-turn instructions.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/directions/)

[Isochrone API\
\
The Mapbox Isochrone API computes areas that are reachable within a specified amount of time from a location.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/isochrone/)

[EV Charge Finder API\
\
BETA\
\
The Mapbox EV Charge Finder API provides a cohesive set of services for EV use cases.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/ev-charge-finder/)

[Map Matching API\
\
The Mapbox Map Matching API snaps fuzzy, inaccurate traces to the road and path network.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/map-matching/)

[Matrix API\
\
The Mapbox Matrix API returns travel times between many points.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/matrix/)

[Optimization API v1\
\
The Mapbox Optimization API returns a duration-optimized route between the input coordinates.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/optimization-v1/)

[Optimization API v2\
\
BETA\
\
The Mapbox Optimization API returns a duration-optimized route between the input coordinates.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/optimization/)

[Using HTTP POST\
\
The Directions API and Map Matching API both support access using the HTTP POST method.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/http-post/)

[Navigation service changelog\
\
This changelog documents all changes made to Navigation service APIs.\
\
chevron-right](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/navigation/changelog/)

# Maps APIs Caching | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Caching[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#caching "Direct link to Caching")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Caching information specific to each of Mapbox's Maps APIs is in the "Restrictions and limits" section near the end of each individual Maps API page. But some tips on working with caching are common across Maps APIs.

### Glossary[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#glossary "Direct link to Glossary")

*   **TTL** (Time To Live): The lifetime of an object before the CDN replaces it with a new version.
*   **CDN** (Content Delivery Network): Globally distributed entry point for all HTTP requests at api.mapbox.com. Our CDN caches responses for defined time intervals to improve performance.
*   **Cache-Control**: The standardized [HTTP header](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Cache-Control)
     for directing cache instructions for requests and responses. Most Maps APIs set both a `max-age` (or device cache TTL) cache-control header and a `s-maxage` (or CDN cache TTL) cache-control header.

### How long does it take for changes to a style or tileset to be reflected in my map?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#how-long-does-it-take-for-changes-to-a-style-or-tileset-to-be-reflected-in-my-map "Direct link to How long does it take for changes to a style or tileset to be reflected in my map?")

Cache times for styles and tilesets depend on whether you're making direct API requests or using a Mapbox SDK. With Mapbox SDKs, you have the option of [invalidating the device cache](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#how-can-i-break-the-device-cache)
.

#### Tileset changes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#tileset-changes "Direct link to Tileset changes")

To find the cache defaults for an API where a tileset is used, you can look at the "Restrictions and limits" section near the end of the individual API page. For example, there will be at least a 5-minute delay to show newly created tiles for an existing tileset used in the Vector Tiles API or Mapbox GL JS. The device TTL for vector tiles is 12 hours — see below for details on [breaking the device cache](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#how-can-i-break-the-device-cache)
.

If an implementation relies on our Static APIs, data updates to cached tiles won't be visible for at least 12 hours due to the CDN cache default. You cannot break the CDN cache.

#### Style changes[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#style-changes "Direct link to Style changes")

After you have published changes to a style, there will be at least a 15-minute delay to see the update reflected in a map. If the map style used in an application was rendered on a device before that style was updated, then the limiting factor may be the vector tile TTL, since the default device TTL is 12 hours — see below for details on [breaking the device cache](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#how-can-i-break-the-device-cache)
.

Any style changes propagating downstream to the Static API won't be visible for at least 12 hours due to the CDN cache default. You cannot break the CDN cache.

### How can I break the device cache?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#how-can-i-break-the-device-cache "Direct link to How can I break the device cache?")

*   To invalidate the device cache with Mapbox SDKs, you can use the relevant methods linked here: [Mapbox Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/maps/api/10.13.0/mapbox-maps-android/com.mapbox.maps/-mapbox-map/#-1587968132%2FFunctions%2F380272269)
    , [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.13.1/Classes/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps0A3MapC9clearData3for10completionyAA15ResourceOptionsV_ys5Error_pSgctFZ)
    , or [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#clearstorage)
    .
*   On the web, you can clear your browser cache via developer tools or browse in incognito mode.

### Can I invalidate the CDN cache?[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/api-caching/#can-i-invalidate-the-cdn-cache "Direct link to Can I invalidate the CDN cache?")

No, the CDN cache for styles and tilesets cannot be invalidated.

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# access token | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/access-token/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

You need a Mapbox **access token** to use any of Mapbox's tools, APIs, or SDKs. Mapbox uses access tokens to associate your account with your requests to Mapbox API resources. You can find all your access tokens, create new ones, or delete existing ones on your [Access tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
. You can also use the [Mapbox Tokens API](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/tokens-api/)
 to programmatically create, update, and delete access tokens.

Access tokens have _token scopes_ that grant access to different actions. Token scopes define which Mapbox APIs can be accessed by a token, as well as which methods can be used to access those APIs. Access tokens have either [public](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#public-scopes)
 or [secret](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#secret-scopes)
 scopes.

Your account will always have at least one public access token. This token is your [_default public token_](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#default-public-access-token)
. When you're logged into your account, this token will automatically be embedded in example code on Mapbox.com.

Here's what an access token looks like in a Mapbox API request (specifically, the [Geocoding API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/search/geocoding/)
):

    https://api.mapbox.com/search/geocode/v6/forward?q=Los%20Angeles&access_token=YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN

**Related resources:**

*   [How do I create an API access token?](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/getting-started/access-tokens/)
    
*   [List of public and private token scopes](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#scopes)
    
*   [Default public access token documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/guides/tokens/#default-public-access-token)
    
*   [Tokens API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/accounts/#tokens)

# Unity SDK | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

Maps SDK for Unity
==================

現在のバージョン：`v2.1.1`[変更履歴を表示](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-unity-sdk/blob/develop/documentation/docs/05-changelog.md)

*   check
    
    Vector maps with 3D buildings
    
*   check
    
    Cloudless satellite base map
    
*   check
    
    Global digital elevation model
    
*   check
    
    Custom data
    

[インストール](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/unity/maps/guides/install/)
[githubGithubで貢献](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-unity-sdk)

The Mapbox Maps SDK for Unity is a collection of tools for building Unity applications from real map data. It enables Unity developers to interact with Mapbox web services APIs and create game objects via a C#-based API and graphical user interface.

Version 2 is no longer in active development

Version 2 of the Maps SDK for Unity is no longer in active development. Version 3 is in development and will be released soon.

This documentation has been reduced to include only an installation guide for modern versions of unity.

Visit the [#unity channel](https://discord.com/channels/1004826913229000704/1092426736215535676)
 in the Mapbox Developer Discord to connect with the user community and get updates on version 3.

Get Started[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/unity/maps/guides/#get-started "Get Started への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ja/unity/maps/guides/install/)
 for instructions on how to install the Maps SDK for Unity and load a map in your scene.

# Manage your web map costs | Help | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/manage-web-map-costs/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This troubleshooting guide shows you different ways to manage your costs for common web map implementations ([interactive](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/manage-web-map-costs/#interactive-maps)
, [non-interactive](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/manage-web-map-costs/#non-interactive-maps)
, and [hybrid](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/manage-web-map-costs/#hybrid-interactive-and-non-interactive-maps)
). The tables in this troubleshooting guide outline common mapping use-cases and offer suggestions for balancing the various cost-effective, performant map options with your ideal end-user experience.

For specific details on how the products discussed in this guide are priced, see the product pricing documentation, linked in each of the following tables.

Note

Every mapping implementation is unique and Mapbox products are optimized for specific use-cases. The way you choose to implement web maps will depend on what end-user experience you want to build. If you're still not sure what is a good option for you after reviewing this documentation, [let us know](https://support.mapbox.com/hc/en-us)
!

Interactive maps[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/manage-web-map-costs/#interactive-maps "Direct link to Interactive maps")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Interactive maps are designed for end-user interaction:

| Use case | Example | Recommendations for cost effective use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Interactive large map | A map-based web application in which the map fills the webpage | Use [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#pricing)<br> (version `1.0.0` or greater), billed by [map load](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/map-loads/)<br>. |
| Limited interaction large or small map | A travel map of cruise destinations in which all lines are visible on initial map load, meaning users don’t need to pan or interact with the map | Use the [Static Tiles API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-tiles/#static-tiles-api-pricing)<br> with any tiling client of your choosing. (For example Leaflet, or OpenLayers. Note that these tiling clients are not actively maintained by Mapbox.) In the tiling client, set the max bounds and the min and max zoom levels to limit the number of tiles loaded. |
| Interactive map below the fold of a webpage | A store locator map anchored at the bottom of a webpage | Use [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#pricing)<br> (version `1.0.0` or greater), billed by [map load](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/map-loads/)<br>. Consider techniques like [lazy loading](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lazy_loading)<br> so that only users who scroll to the map instantiate a map load. |

Non-interactive maps[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/manage-web-map-costs/#non-interactive-maps "Direct link to Non-interactive maps")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Non-interactive static maps are designed to be passively viewed by end-users:

| Use case | Example | Recommendations for cost effective use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Zero-interaction large map | A colorful map used as the backdrop for a website, or a large map image | Use the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/#static-images-api-pricing)<br>. |
| Small map in the corner of a page | A small non-interactive map on a real estate page | Use the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/#static-images-api-pricing)<br>. |
| Data visualizations using client-side data | Showing different non-interactive styles or layers to different subsets of end-users | Use the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/#static-images-api-pricing)<br> with [style parameters](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/#style-parameters)<br>. |

Hybrid, interactive and non-interactive maps[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/troubleshooting/manage-web-map-costs/#hybrid-interactive-and-non-interactive-maps "Direct link to Hybrid, interactive and non-interactive maps")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hybrid maps are designed to display a non-interactive preview that leads to an interactive experience:

| Use case | Example | Recommendations for cost effective use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No map on load, with a text option to **Load Map** | A map of store or service locations | Use [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#pricing)<br> (version `1.0.0` or greater) behind a **Load Map** button to delay initialization of the map or with [lazy loading](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lazy_loading)<br>. |
| A static map on load that opens interactive map when clicked | A hiking website that has text descriptions with a **Load Map** feature that allows end users to interact further with routes | Use the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/#static-images-api-pricing)<br> for non-interactive the map images (thumbnails). Use [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#pricing)<br> (version `1.0.0` or greater), billed by [map load](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/map-loads/)<br>, for the interactive map. |
| Multiple maps on one page | An end-user can see multiple running routes at a glance, and clicking one of the non-interactive map thumbnails opens a larger interactive map | Use the [Static Images API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/static-images/#static-images-api-pricing)<br> for non-interactive the map images (thumbnails). Use [Mapbox GL JS](https://docs.mapbox.com/mapbox-gl-js/api/#pricing)<br> (version `1.0.0` or greater), billed by [map load](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/map-loads/)<br>, for the interactive map. With Mapbox GL JS version `2.0.0` or greater, each map on the same page is billed individually by [map load](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/glossary/map-loads/)<br>. |

Was this page helpful?YesNo

# Mapbox Satellite | Tilesets | Mapbox Docs | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This tileset reference document includes information to help you use the data in the Mapbox Satellite tileset.

Overview[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#overview "Direct link to Overview")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Mapbox Satellite** is a Mapbox-provided raster tileset that includes a global basemap of high resolution satellite and aerial imagery. The tileset contains one complete composite imagery layer with color-corrected imagery that is optimized for a balance between recency and aesthetic quality.

This Mapbox Satellite tileset is the raster data source for the [Mapbox-owned styles](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/maps/styles/#mapbox-styles)
 _Mapbox Satellite_ and _Mapbox Satellite Streets_.

To learn more about Mapbox imagery, read the [Imagery and raster data](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/guides/imagery/)
 guide.

### Data sources[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#data-sources "Direct link to Data sources")

Mapbox Satellite imagery comes from a [variety of sources](https://www.mapbox.com/about/maps#faq-mapbox-satellite)
, including commercial providers and open data from NASA, USGS, and others. The raw imagery is color-corrected to look realistic and blended together into a single raster tileset.

Data sources differ depending on zoom level and geographic availability.

*   **Zoom levels 0 – 8** use [de-clouded](https://blog.mapbox.com/making-clouds-go-away-on-mapbox-satellite-82a3450af515)
     data from NASA MODIS satellites.

*   **Zoom levels 9 – 12** use primarily [Maxar](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/30-meter-imagery-update-improved-recency-color-and-resolution-for-seamless-3d-terrain)
     satellite imagery and [NASA/USGS Landsat 5 & 7](https://blog.mapbox.com/open-aerial-the-data-behind-mapbox-satellite-ed890628f2e5)
     imagery in limited locations.
*   **Zoom levels 13-16** use [Maxar's Vivid product](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/3d-satellite-maps-high-res-imagery)
     for the majority of global coverage.
*   **Zoom levels 16+** use [Vexcel aerial imagery](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/updated-aerial-imagery-for-the-u-s-canada-and-europe)
     over North American - and some European - cities, and open aerial imagery from the Netherlands, Denmark, France, Germany, Chechia, Poland, Spain, New Zealand, and other regions.

Clockwise from top left: MODIS, Landsat, Maxar satellite, Vexcel aerial

### Data updates[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#data-updates "Direct link to Data updates")

Imagery is not improved on a set schedule and is updated when and where it becomes available. New data will be automatically added, so you will not need to change your implementations to access it.

To view the latest updates to satellite data, see the [Changelog](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#changelog)
 below.

### Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#attribution "Direct link to Attribution")

When using this tileset publicly in a design or application you must provide [proper attribution](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/how-mapbox-works/attribution/)
.

Layer Reference[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#layer-reference "Direct link to Layer Reference")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This tileset includes global coverage to zoom 16 (1–2 m resolution), regional coverage to zoom 18 (0.6–0.3 m resolution), and select coverage to zoom 21+ (7.5 cm+).

*   **One composite layer** with global imagery and metadata.
*   **A base of 50 cm imagery** for the world (excluding Antarctica and Greenland).
*   **Higher resolution imagery** in certain areas in the US, Canada, Europe, and Australia/NZ.

Use this imagery[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#use-this-imagery "Direct link to Use this imagery")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are several ways you can use Mapbox Satellite in your projects:

*   Add it as a template style in [Mapbox Studio](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/reference/styles/#pick-a-template)
    .
*   [Add Mapbox Satellite as a layer in Mapbox Studio](https://docs.mapbox.com/studio-manual/reference/styles/#new-layer)
    .
*   Use Mapbox Satellite through any of [Mapbox's APIs and SDKs](https://docs.mapbox.com/)
     with the tileset ID `mapbox.satellite`.

Changelog[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/data/tilesets/reference/mapbox-satellite/#changelog "Direct link to Changelog")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Apr 23, 2024 - [Updated Aerial Imagery For The US, Canada, And Europe](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/updated-aerial-imagery-for-the-u-s-canada-and-europe)
    
*   Aug 15, 2023 - [1M Square Kilometers Of Updated Satellite Imagery In Europe And Brazil](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/updated-satellite-imagery-europe-brazil)
    
*   Mar 1, 2022 - [Updated 50cm Imagery In UK, Italy, Finland, Japan, India, And China](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/updated-50-cm-imagery)
    
*   Oct 26, 2021 - [Updated 50cm Imagery Covering Israel And Palestine](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/new-israel-palestine-imagery)
    
*   Sep 22, 2021 - [New Aerial Imagery For Germany And Austria](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/new-germany-austria-imagery)
    
*   Aug 19, 2021 - [New Aerial Imagery For France And Portugal](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/new-aerial-imagery-for-france-and-portugal)
    
*   Jun 28, 2021 - [New Imagery for the Netherlands and Switzerland](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/new-imagery-for-the-netherlands-and-switzerland)
    
*   Mar 30, 2021 - [30+ Meter Imagery Update: Improved Recency, Color, And Resolution For Seamless 3D](https://www.mapbox.com/blog/30-meter-imagery-update-improved-recency-color-and-resolution-for-seamless-3d-terrain)

# Navigation SDK v1 | iOS | Mapbox

[Skip to main content](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK for iOS v1
=========================

Current version:`v1.4.2`[View changelog](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    A navigation UI ready to drop into your application
    
*   check
    
    Professionally designed map styles
    
*   check
    
    Worldwide driving, cycling, and walking directions
    
*   check
    
    Traffic avoidance and proactive rerouting
    

[githubContribute on GitHub](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios)

A newer version of the Navigation SDK is available

This page uses v1.4.2 of the Mapbox Navigation SDK. A newer version of the SDK is available. Learn about the latest version, v2.20.0, in the [Navigation SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/guides/)
.

The Mapbox Navigation SDK gives you all the tools you need to add turn-by-turn navigation to your application. It takes only a few minutes to drop a full-fledged turn-by-turn navigation view controller into your application. Or use the Core Navigation framework directly to build something truly custom.

Structure of the SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/guides/#structure-of-the-sdk "Direct link to Structure of the SDK")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK is divided into two frameworks: the **Mapbox Core Navigation framework** (`MapboxCoreNavigation`) and the **Mapbox Navigation framework** (`MapboxNavigation`).

### Mapbox Core Navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/guides/#mapbox-core-navigation "Direct link to Mapbox Core Navigation")

The **Mapbox Core Navigation framework** (`MapboxCoreNavigation`) contains the logic that allows you to receive user location updates, determine their relation to the route line, and get notifications that show the current state of the application in the form of a [`RouteProgress`](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/api/1.4.2/Classes/RouteProgress.html)
 object.

You can use `MapboxCoreNavigation` directly in your project to build a completely custom navigation UI or use it via the **Mapbox Navigation framework** (`MapboxNavigation`), which is powered by `MapboxCoreNavigation`.

### Mapbox Navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/guides/#mapbox-navigation "Direct link to Mapbox Navigation")

The **Mapbox Navigation framework** (`MapboxNavigation`) is the ready-made turn-by-turn navigation user interface (UI). Use `MapboxNavigation` to get up and running quickly with an in-app navigation experience. With a few lines of code, add a drop-in UI, automatic day-to-night map switching, and automatic rerouting in traffic. Adjust the style of various elements of the ready-made UI, react to location-related events as they occur, and adjust various aspects of the map view’s appearance.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/guides/#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK and Core Navigation are compatible with applications written in Swift 5 in Xcode 10.2 and above. The Mapbox Navigation and Mapbox Core Navigation frameworks run on iOS 10.0 and above.

Available resources[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/guides/#available-resources "Direct link to Available resources")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For further details, consult the guides and examples included with this API reference. Documentation for the Mapbox Navigation SDK for iOS comes in the form of:

*   [API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/navigation/api/1.4.2/)
    
*   [Examples](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/legacy/navigation/examples/)
    
*   [Tutorials and troubleshooting guides](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/)
    

We welcome your [bug reports, feature requests, and contributions](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
. If you can't find what you're looking for, [reach out](https://www.mapbox.com/contact/support/)
 to our support team.

---

*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/MapInitOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/MapView.html)

    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GestureManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14GestureManagerC) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/GestureManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/AppleLocationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/Location.html)

*   `[LocationManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)LocationManager) `
    
    An object responsible for notifying the map view about location-related events, such as a change in the device’s location.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/LocationManager.html)

*   `[Style](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/s:10MapboxMaps5StyleC) `

    #### Declaration
    
*   `[CompassDirectionFormatter](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/s:10MapboxMaps25CompassDirectionFormatterC) `
    
    The `CompassDirectionFormatter` class provides properly formatted descriptions of absolute compass directions. For example, a value of `90` may be formatted as “east”, depending on the locale.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/CompassDirectionFormatter.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)OrnamentsManager) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/OrnamentsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### Snapshotter](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/Snapshotter)

*   `[Snapshotter](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes.html#/s:10MapboxMaps11SnapshotterC) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.0.0/Classes/Snapshotter.html)

# Navigation SDK v2 | iOS | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Navigation SDK for iOS
======================

現在のバージョン：`v2.20.0`[変更履歴を表示](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

*   check
    
    Navigation UI components ready to integrate into your application
    
*   check
    
    Professionally designed map styles
    
*   check
    
    Worldwide driving, cycling, and walking directions
    
*   check
    
    Traffic avoidance and proactive rerouting
    

[githubGithubで貢献](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-navigation-ios)

A newer version of the Navigation SDK is available

This page uses 2.20.0 of the Mapbox Navigation SDK. A newer version of the SDK is available. Learn about the latest version, 3.10.1, in the [Navigation SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/guides/)
.

The Navigation SDK for iOS allows you to build a complete navigation experience with the power of the [Mapbox Directions API](https://docs.mapbox.com/api/ja/navigation/directions/)
. You can build a complete turn-by-turn navigation UI inside your application using our pre-built components or use the core logic directly to build something truly custom.

The Navigation SDK provides a collection of features that are critical when building navigation projects, including:

*   Navigation routes
*   Accurate device location updates
*   Voice instruction announcements
*   Real-time user progress to their destination
*   Off-route detection and rerouting
*   Dynamic camera adjustments during turn-by-turn navigation
*   Pre-built UI components to show changing navigation information
*   Support for navigating with limited or no network connection

Types of navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#types-of-navigation "Types of navigation への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Navigation SDK offers two types of navigation: turn-by-turn navigation and free-drive navigation.

### Turn-by-turn navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#turn-by-turn-navigation "Turn-by-turn navigation への直接リンク")

Turn-by-turn navigation, also referred to as _active guidance_, is likely what comes to mind when you think about a typical navigation application: a user receives turn-by-turn instructions as they progress along the route. Read more in the [Turn-by-turn navigation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/turn-by-turn-navigation/)
 guides.

### Free-drive navigation[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#free-drive-navigation "Free-drive navigation への直接リンク")

Free-drive navigation, also referred to as _passive navigation_, is a unique Mapbox Navigation SDK feature that allows drivers to take advantage of some navigation features without selecting a route and following turn-by-turn instructions to a set destination. Read more in the [Free-drive navigation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/free-drive/)
 guide.

Requirements[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#requirements "Requirements への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is compatible with applications that are:

*   Built in Xcode 13.3.1 or higher
*   Written in Swift 5.6 or higher
*   Run on iOS 12 or higher

Use with other Mapbox tools[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#use-with-other-mapbox-tools "Use with other Mapbox tools への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mapbox Navigation SDK for iOS is designed to be used with several other Mapbox products.

### Directions API[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#directions-api "Directions API への直接リンク")

The Navigation SDK uses the Mapbox Directions API to generate routes between two or more coordinates. The Mapbox Directions for Swift framework has many route options like setting a profile identifier (for example driving, driving with traffic, or walking), waypoint heading (to influence the direction in which a route will begin), and `roadClassesToAvoid` (for example, avoiding tolls). Read more about Mapbox [Directions for Swift in the API documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/directions/api/2.14.1/)
.

### Maps SDK for iOS[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#maps-sdk-for-ios "Maps SDK for iOS への直接リンク")

By default, the Navigation SDK comes with access to the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS. The Navigation SDK uses the Maps SDK to display routes on a map and show the user's location on the map throughout their trip to provide both navigation-related context and nearby places that may be of interest to the driver. The Navigation SDK uses Mapbox Maps SDK v10, you can find documentation for your version in the [Maps SDK API references listing](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/api-reference/)
.

Conditions[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#conditions "Conditions への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Attribution[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#attribution "Attribution への直接リンク")

If you use a Mapbox map in your application, you are required to provide attribution according to the [Mapbox Maps SDK documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/#attribution)
.

### Terms[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#terms "Terms への直接リンク")

The Mapbox Navigation SDK is distributed under [Mapbox's Terms of Service](https://www.mapbox.com/legal/tos)
.

### Pricing[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#pricing "Pricing への直接リンク")

Read more about pricing for the Navigation SDK in the [pricing guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/pricing/)
.

Older versions of the SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/v2/guides/#older-versions-of-the-sdk "Older versions of the SDK への直接リンク")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Looking for an older version of the Navigation SDK? See the [Navigation SDK v1 documentation](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/legacy/navigation/guides/)
.

# View Annotations  Reference

[View Annotations\
================](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/View%20Annotations.html#/View%20Annotations)

*   `[ViewAnnotationManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationManagerC) `
    
    Manager API to control View Annotations.
    
    View annotations are `UIView` instances that are drawn on top of the `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/Classes/MapView.html) ` and bound to some `[Geometry](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/GeoJSON.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8Geometrya) ` (only `Point` is supported for now). In case some view annotations intersect on the screen Z-index is based on addition order.
    
    View annotations are invariant to map camera transformations however such properties as size, visibility etc could be controlled by the user using update operation.
    
    View annotations are not explicitly bound to any sources however `[associatedFeatureId](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/Structs/ViewAnnotationOptions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationOptionsV19associatedFeatureIdSSSgvp) ` could be used to bind given view annotation with some `[Feature](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/GeoJSON.html#/s:10MapboxMaps7Featurea) ` by `Feature.identifier` meaning visibility of view annotation will be driven by visibility of given feature.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/Classes/ViewAnnotationManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewAnnotationUpdateObserver](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28ViewAnnotationUpdateObserverP) `
    
    An interface you use to detect when the map view lays out or updates visibility of annotation views.
    
    When visible portion of a map changes, e.g. responding to the user interaction, the map view adjusts the positions and visibility of its annotation views. Implement methods of `ViewAnnotationUpdateObserver` to detect when the map view updates position/size for supplied annotation views. As well as when annotation views get show/hidden when going in/out of visible portion of the map.
    
    To register an observer for view annotation updates, call the `[addViewAnnotationUpdateObserver(_:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/Classes/ViewAnnotationManager.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationManagerC03addcD14UpdateObserveryyAA0cdgH0_pF) ` method.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/Protocols/ViewAnnotationUpdateObserver.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewAnnotationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationOptionsV) `
    
    Stores layout and visibilty settings for a `ViewAnnotation`
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/Structs/ViewAnnotationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewAnnotationManagerError](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps26ViewAnnotationManagerErrorO) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.1/Enums/ViewAnnotationManagerError.html)
    
    #### Declaration
