# Ornaments  Reference

[Ornaments\
=========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/Ornaments)

*   `[OrnamentsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)OrnamentsManager) `
    
    APIs for managing map ornaments
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Classes/OrnamentsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15OrnamentOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure Ornament-specific capabilities of the map
    
    All margin values are relative to the `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Classes/MapView.html) `‘s safe area. To allow the safe area (and thereby ornaments) to track the presence of navigation bars and tab bars, make MapView the root view of a view controller.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Structs/OrnamentOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentPosition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps16OrnamentPositionO) `
    
    Options used to configure the corner position of an ornament
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Enums/OrnamentPosition.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentVisibility](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18OrnamentVisibilityO) `
    
    Options used to configure the visibility of an ornament
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Enums/OrnamentVisibility.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionButtonOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps24AttributionButtonOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure position, margin, and visibility for the map’s attribution button.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Structs/AttributionButtonOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[CompassViewOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18CompassViewOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure position, margin, image, and visibility for the map’s compass view.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Structs/CompassViewOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[LogoViewOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15LogoViewOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure position, margin, and visibility for the map’s logo view.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Structs/LogoViewOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ScaleBarViewOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps19ScaleBarViewOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure position, margin, and visibility for the map’s scale bar view.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.3/Structs/ScaleBarViewOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration

# Troubleshooting Mapbox Maps and Navigation SDKs for iOS installation | Help | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Learn how to troubleshoot issues when installing the Mapbox Maps or Navigation SDK for iOS with Swift Package Manager, CocoaPods, and Carthage.

General troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#general-troubleshooting "General troubleshooting への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you see `401` responses after your app builds successfully, make sure that you've [configured all credentials correctly](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/#configure-credentials)
. Note that installing Maps SDK v6.0.0, Navigation SDK v1.0.0, or Search SDK v1.0.0 or greater via Carthage or CocoaPods requires **two access tokens**: one to download the SDK and another to authenticate map requests.

Swift Package Manager[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#swift-package-manager "Swift Package Manager への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Swift Package Manager (SPM) is the recommended method for installing the Maps SDK. If you run into issues while installing the framework with SPM, try following these steps:

*   Xcode may fail to resolve SPM artifacts with errors such as `artifact of binary target 'MapboxCommon' failed extraction: The operation couldn’t be completed. (TSCBasic.StringError error 1.)`. If these errors occur, try resetting the SPM cache by opening the **File** menu in Xcode and selecting **Swift Packages** > **Reset Package Cache**. You may also need to delete your derived data folder.

CocoaPods[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#cocoapods "CocoaPods への直接リンク")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Though Swift Package Manager is the preferred method for installing the Maps SDK, if you're already using CocoaPods to manage other dependencies in your project, you can also install the Mapbox SDKs for iOS in the same way.

If you run into issues while installing the framework with CocoaPods, try following these steps:

*   Use the most recent version of CocoaPods. Beta versions are not recommended for use with the Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS.
*   If `pod install` gives you an “Unable to find a specification” error, try running `pod repo update` first.
*   When you run `pod install`, CocoaPods generates an `xcworkspace` file that includes its configuration. The `xcworkspace` file has a white Xcode icon, unlike the `xcodeproj` file which has a blue Xcode icon. Make sure you're opening this file and not the original Xcode project.
*   If you're getting a permission error or having trouble installing CocoaPods itself, consult the [CocoaPods troubleshooting](https://guides.cocoapods.org/using/troubleshooting.html)
     documentation.

If you're still having issues using Mapbox with CocoaPods, [contact support](https://www.mapbox.com/contact)
 with the complete details of the issue, including error messages.

### Mapbox Maps SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-maps-sdk "Mapbox Maps SDK への直接リンク")

*   If you see the error `Mapbox.h not found`, this can typically be resolved by building your application despite the error.
    
*   If you're using version v6.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Maps SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        [!] Error installing MapboxMapscurl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 401 Unauthorized
    
    To resolve this, create a secret access token from the [Access Token page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     in your Developer Console, and follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/)
     to configure your secret token.
    

### Mapbox Navigation SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-navigation-sdk "Mapbox Navigation SDK への直接リンク")

*   If you're using version v1.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Navigation SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        [!] Error installing MapboxNavigationcurl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 401 Unauthorized
    
    To resolve this, create a secret access token from the [Access Token page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     in your Developer Console, and follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/overview/#installation)
     to configure your secret token.
    

Carthage[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#carthage "Carthage への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Mapbox Maps SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-maps-sdk-1 "Mapbox Maps SDK への直接リンク")

#### v10.0.0 and higher[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#v1000-and-higher "v10.0.0 and higher への直接リンク")

Installation via Carthage is not supported in the Maps SDK for iOS v10.0.0 and higher.

#### Less than v10.0.0[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#less-than-v1000 "Less than v10.0.0 への直接リンク")

If you run into issues while installing the framework with Carthage, try following these steps:

*   Make sure you're using Carthage version 0.35.0 or above.
    
*   If you are encountering an error message like the one below for any Mapbox framework:
    
        *** Downloading binary-only framework MapboxMaps at "https://api.mapbox.com/downloads/v2/mobile-maps-ios/releases/ios/{VERSION_NUMBER}/MapboxMaps.zip"No available version for binary "https://s3.amazonaws.com/mapbox-ios/accounts/MapboxAccounts.json" satisfies the requirement: ~> 2.3.0
    
    You may need to verify that the dependency version specified in your `Cartfile` is correct, and then reset the `Carthage` cache:
    
        rm -rf Carthage &&rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/carthage &&rm -rf ~/Library/Caches/org.carthage.CarthageKit
    

*   If you're using version v6.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Maps SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        A shell task (/usr/bin/env unzip -uo -qq -d .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip) failed with exit code 9:[.@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip]End-of-central-directory signature not found.  Either this file is nota zipfile, or it constitutes one disk of a multi-part archive.  In thelatter case the central directory and zipfile comment will be found onthe last disk(s) of this archive.unzip:  cannot find zipfile directory in one of .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip or      .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.zip, and cannot find .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.ZIP, period.
    
    To resolve this, create a secret access token from the [Access Token page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
     in your Developer Console, and follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/install/)
     to configure your secret token.
    
*   If you continue to run into the same error after including your `~/.netrc` file, try resetting your Carthage cache and try installing again. See cache resetting instructions above.
    

### Mapbox Navigation SDK[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#mapbox-navigation-sdk-1 "Mapbox Navigation SDK への直接リンク")

#### v2.0.0 and higher[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#v200-and-higher "v2.0.0 and higher への直接リンク")

`MapboxNavigation` no longer supports Carthage as of v2.0.0. If you are building a user interface from scratch, you can still install only the `MapboxCoreNavigation` framework using Carthage.

#### Less than v2.0.0[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#less-than-v200 "Less than v2.0.0 への直接リンク")

*   If `Carthage` reports an error like this:
    
        Build Failed   Task failed with exit code 70:   /usr/bin/xcrun xcodebuild <...>   This usually indicates that project itself failed to compile. Check the xcodebuild log for more details: /var/folders/cx/6_wt73h5365_j0ty6085kg2m0000gn/T/carthage-xcodebuild.OahRgR.log
    
    Check that you included the `--platform ios` key in your `carthage update` command:
    
        % carthage update --platform ios --use-netrc
    

*   If you are using version v1.0.0 of the SDK or later, you will need to authorize your download of the Navigation SDK with a secret access token with the `DOWNLOADS:READ` scope. Otherwise, you will see the following error:
    
        A shell task (/usr/bin/env unzip -uo -qq -d.@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip) failed with exit code 9:[.@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip]End-of-central-directory signature not found.  Either this file is nota zipfile, or it constitutes one disk of a multi-part archive.  In thelatter case the central directory and zipfile comment will be found onthe last disk(s) of this archive.unzip:  cannot find zipfile directory in one of .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip or      .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.zip, and cannot find .@site/src/mapbox-ios-sdk-dynamic.zip.ZIP, period.
    
    To resolve this, follow the instructions outlined in the [installation guide](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/navigation/overview/#installation)
     to configure your secret token.
    
*   If you continue to run into the same error after including your `~/.netrc` file, try resetting your Carthage cache and try installing again. See cache resetting instructions above.
    

CI troubleshooting[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/ios-sdk-installation/#ci-troubleshooting "CI troubleshooting への直接リンク")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you use a dependency manager and automate builds on CI, you need to update your CI configuration. Below are the instructions to integrate the `.netrc` file into your CI:

*   **A secret access token with the `Downloads:Read` scope**.
    1.  From your account's [tokens page](https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/)
        , click the **Create a token** button.
    2.  From the token creation page, give your token a name and make sure the box next to the `Downloads:Read` scope is checked.
    3.  Click the **Create token** button at the bottom of the page to create your token.
    4.  The token you've created is a _secret token_, which means you will only have one opportunity to copy it somewhere secure.
*   Add an `SDK_REGISTRY_TOKEN` environment variable to your CI system of choice.
*   In your CI configuration file (or in a Script step), insert a new run step before fetching dependencies to the build job which runs the following commands:
    
        echo "machine api.mapbox.comlogin mapboxpassword ${SDK_REGISTRY_TOKEN}" >~/.netrc
    
*   If the project uses Carthage, change the carthage bootstrap invocation to pass in `--use-netrc`. Add this flag to the Additional Options field of the Carthage step.
*   Note that the secret access token in the `~/.netrc` file is sensitive and should not be exposed in any public CI logs. Refer to your continuous integration provider’s documentation on how to keep your secret access token from appearing in public logs.

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

---

# Keep access tokens private in open source iOS and Android apps | Help | Mapbox

[メインコンテンツまでスキップ](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/private-access-token-android-and-ios/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

An app using the [Mapbox Maps SDK for iOS](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/ja/maps/guides/)
 or the [Mapbox Maps SDK for Android](https://docs.mapbox.com/android/ja/maps/guides/)
, must provide an [access token](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/glossary/access-token/)
 to display Mapbox-hosted maps and use other Mapbox services.

It is important to practice responsible token management with all Mapbox access tokens. This guide expands on general [token management](https://docs.mapbox.com/accounts/ja/guides/tokens/#creating-and-managing-access-tokens)
 strategies to provide guidance specific to iOS and Android applications.

iOS[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/private-access-token-android-and-ios/#ios "iOS への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Create a new plain text file containing your access token, named either `.mapbox` or `mapbox`. To avoid accidentally committing this file to an open-source project, either you can save it to a location outside your project's version-controlled directory, or you can add this file to your project’s [.gitignore](https://help.github.com/articles/ignoring-files/)
     file.
2.  Open your project in Xcode. In the project editor, go to the Build Phases tab, then click the + button to add a new Run Script phase to the end.
3.  Customize the Run Script build phase to run the following code (replacing `~/.mapbox` or `~/mapbox` with the path to the file you added in step 1):
    
        # Look for a global file named 'mapbox' or '.mapbox' within the home directorytoken_file=~/.mapboxtoken_file2=~/mapboxtoken="$(cat $token_file 2>/dev/null || cat $token_file2 2>/dev/null)"if [ "$token" ]; then  plutil -replace MBXAccessToken -string $token "$TARGET_BUILD_DIR/$INFOPLIST_PATH"else  echo 'warning: Missing Mapbox access token'  open 'https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/'  echo "warning: Get an access token from <https://console.mapbox.com/account/access-tokens/>, then create a new file at $token_file or $token_file2 that contains the access token."fi
    
4.  Add `$(TARGET_BUILD_DIR)/$(INFOPLIST_PATH)` to the build phase’s Input Files section. Otherwise, the access token may be overridden during incremental builds. Optionally, you can also add `~/.mapbox` or `~/mapbox` to this section, so that Xcode will automatically update Info.plist after you change your access token.

When building the project in Xcode, the access token will be inserted into the Info.plist inside your built app, but not into the Info.plist that you’d commit.

Android[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/private-access-token-android-and-ios/#android "Android への直接リンク")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Git-based option[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/private-access-token-android-and-ios/#git-based-option "Git-based option への直接リンク")

Be aware that to enable continuous integration builds with the following approach, you can take advantage of [a Gradle script that the Mapbox Android team uses itself](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-native-android/blob/master/gradle/gradle-config.gradle)
.

1.  [Create a local `.gitignore` file in your repository folder](https://help.github.com/articles/ignoring-files/)
    . If the folder already has a `.gitignore` file, locate and open it.
2.  Add the following line to the `.gitignore` file : `YOUR_APP_MODULE_NAME/src/main/res/values/developer-config.xml`. This will tell the Git system to ignore the XML values file that you will create in the next step.
3.  Navigate to your project's `src/main/res/values` folder and make a new file titled `developer-config.xml`.
4.  Create an `mapbox_access_token` String resource and paste your access token into it:

    <string name="mapbox_access_token">PASTE_YOUR_TOKEN_HERE</string>

5.  At this point, the `R.string.mapbox_access_token` string resource should be discoverable wherever you deliver the access token to the `Mapbox` class such as: `Mapbox.getInstance(context, getString(R.string.mapbox_access_token));`
6.  Because of step #3 above, the Git system shouldn't be tracking the `developer-config.xml` file at all.
7.  You're all set! You can use and change the Mapbox access token locally on your machine without the chance of committing and pushing it to a public platform such as GitHub!

### Non-Git option[​](https://docs.mapbox.com/help/ja/troubleshooting/private-access-token-android-and-ios/#non-git-option "Non-Git option への直接リンク")

1.  Find or create a `gradle.properties` file in your Gradle user home folder. The folder is located at `«USER_HOME»/.gradle`. Once the file is created, its path should be `«USER_HOME»/.gradle/gradle.properties`. [More information about Gradle properties](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/build_environment.html#sec:gradle_configuration_properties)
    .
2.  Add a key-value pair to the `gradle.properties` file: `MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN="PASTE_YOUR_TOKEN_HERE";`
3.  Open your app-level `build.gradle` file. This file is usually the one where you define specific dependencies for your app, such as the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android.
4.  Add `MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN` as a `buildConfigField` entry to whichever buildTypes your app has:

    android {	buildTypes {	  debug {	    ...	    buildConfigField 'String', "MapboxAccessToken", MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN	    ...	  }	  release {	  ...	    buildConfigField 'String', "MapboxAccessToken", MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN	  ...	  }	}}

5\. Because your Gradle files have changed, make sure to sync the project with the latest Gradle files. 6. Now you can access your Mapbox access token in your app to initialize the Mapbox Maps SDK for Android: `Mapbox.getInstance(context, BuildConfig.MapboxAccessToken);`

このpageは役に立ちましたか？はいいいえ

# Utilities  Reference

[Utilities\
=========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Utilities.html#/Utilities)

*   `[CompassDirectionFormatter](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Utilities.html#/s:10MapboxMaps25CompassDirectionFormatterC) `
    
    The `CompassDirectionFormatter` class provides properly formatted descriptions of absolute compass directions. For example, a value of `90` may be formatted as “east”, depending on the locale.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/CompassDirectionFormatter.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Projection](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Utilities.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10ProjectionC) `
    
    A collection of [Spherical Mercator](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_Mercator_projection)
     projection methods.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.2/Classes/Projection.html)
    
    #### Declaration

# Style  Reference

[Style\
=====](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/Style)

*   `[Style](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps5StyleC) `
    
    Style provides access to the APIs used to dynamically modify the map’s style. Use it to read and write layers, sources, and images. Obtain the Style instance for a MapView via MapView.mapboxMap.style.
    
    Note: Style should only be used from the main thread.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Classes/Style.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[StyleURI](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8StyleURIV) `
    
    Enum representing the latest version of the Mapbox styles (as of publication). In addition, you can provide a custom URL or earlier version of a Mapbox style by using the `.custom` case.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Structs/StyleURI.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[LayerPosition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13LayerPositionO) `
    
    Specifies the position at which a layer will be added when using `Style.addLayer`.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Enums/LayerPosition.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[TransitionOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMTransitionOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Extensions/TransitionOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Light](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps5LightV) `
    
    The global light source.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Structs/Light.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Terrain](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps7TerrainV) `
    
    The global terrain source.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Structs/Terrain.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[StyleDecodable](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14StyleDecodableP) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Protocols/StyleDecodable.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[StyleEncodable](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14StyleEncodableP) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Protocols/StyleEncodable.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### Style error types](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/Style%20error%20types)

*   `[StyleError](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10StyleErrorV) `
    
    Type of errors thrown by the `[Style](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Classes/Style.html) ` APIs.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.3/Structs/StyleError.html)

# Other  Reference

[Other\
=====](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.17.0/Other.html#/Other)

*   `[Cancelable](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.17.0/Other.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10CancelableP) `
    
    A type that conforms to `Cancelable` typically represents a long running operation that can be canceled. Custom implementations must behave correctly if `[cancel()](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.17.0/Protocols/Cancelable.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10CancelableP6cancelyyF) ` is invoked more than once.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.17.0/Protocols/Cancelable.html)

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Extensions/CoordinateBounds.html)

*   `[Event](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps5Eventa) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### MapEvents](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/MapEvents)

*   `[MapEvents](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapEvents) `
    
    List of supported event types by the MapView and Snapshotter objects, and event data format specification for each event.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Extensions/MapEvents.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[RenderedQueryOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMRenderedQueryOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Extensions/RenderedQueryOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[QueriedFeature](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMQueriedFeature) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Extensions/QueriedFeature.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[FeatureExtensionValue](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMFeatureExtensionValue) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Extensions/FeatureExtensionValue.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### Style error types](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/Style%20error%20types)

*   `[MapError](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8MapErrorV) `
    
    Type of errors thrown by the `[MapboxMap](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Classes/MapboxMap.html) ` APIs.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Structs/MapError.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapLoadingError](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15MapLoadingErrorO) `
    
    Error type that represents the data returned with the `.mapLoadingError` event
    
    The associated message (which is returned by `errorDescription`) contains a descriptive error message.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Enums/MapLoadingError.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapProjection](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13MapProjectionO) `
    
    Describes the projection used to render the map.
    
    Mapbox map supports Mercator and Globe projections.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Enums/MapProjection.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlobeMapProjection](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18GlobeMapProjectionV) `
    
    Globe projection is a custom map projection mode for rendering the map wrapped around a full 3D globe. Conceptually it is the undistorted and unskewed “ground truth” view of the map that preserves true proportions between different areas of the map.
    
    Some layers are not supported when map is in Globe projection:
    
    *   custom
    *   fill extrusion
    *   location indicator
    
    If Globe projection is set it will be switched automatically to Mercator projection when passing `[GlobeMapProjection.transitionZoomLevel](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Structs/GlobeMapProjection.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18GlobeMapProjectionV19transitionZoomLevel12CoreGraphics7CGFloatVvpZ) ` during zooming in.
    
    See `[GlobeMapProjection.transitionZoomLevel](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Structs/GlobeMapProjection.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18GlobeMapProjectionV19transitionZoomLevel12CoreGraphics7CGFloatVvpZ) ` for more details what projection will be used depending on current zoom level.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Structs/GlobeMapProjection.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MercatorMapProjection](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21MercatorMapProjectionV) `
    
    Mercator projection.
    
    Mercator projection description: [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercator\_projection](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercator_projection)
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Structs/MercatorMapProjection.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapProjectionError](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18MapProjectionErrorO) `
    
    Errors related to MapProjection API
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.0/Enums/MapProjectionError.html)
    
    #### Declaration

---

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.5.0/Enums/Scheme.html)

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.5.0/Enums/Encoding.html)

*   `[SkyLayer](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.1/Layers.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8SkyLayerV) `

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.1/Structs/SkyLayer.html)

# MapboxMap  Reference

[MapboxMap\
=========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/MapboxMap.html#/MapboxMap)

*   `[MapboxMap](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps0A3MapC) `
    
    MapboxMap provides access to the map model, including the camera, style, observable map events, and querying rendered features. Obtain the MapboxMap instance for a MapView via MapView.mapboxMap.
    
    Note: MapboxMap should only be used from the main thread.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/Classes/MapboxMap.html)

*   `[GlobeMapProjection](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18GlobeMapProjectionV) `
    
    Globe projection is a custom map projection mode for rendering the map wrapped around a full 3D globe. Conceptually it is the undistorted and unskewed “ground truth” view of the map that preserves true proportions between different areas of the map.
    
    Some layers are not supported when map is in Globe projection:
    
    *   custom
    *   fill extrusion
    *   location indicator
    
    If Globe projection is set it will be switched automatically to Mercator projection when passing `[GlobeMapProjection.transitionZoomLevel](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/Structs/GlobeMapProjection.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18GlobeMapProjectionV19transitionZoomLevel12CoreGraphics7CGFloatVvpZ) ` during zooming in.
    
    See `[GlobeMapProjection.transitionZoomLevel](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/Structs/GlobeMapProjection.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18GlobeMapProjectionV19transitionZoomLevel12CoreGraphics7CGFloatVvpZ) ` for more details what projection will be used depending on current zoom level.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/Structs/GlobeMapProjection.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MercatorMapProjection](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.4.1/MapboxMap.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21MercatorMapProjectionV) `

# Ornaments  Reference

[Ornaments\
=========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Ornaments.html#/Ornaments)

*   `[OrnamentsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Ornaments.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)OrnamentsManager) `
    
    APIs for managing map ornaments
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/OrnamentsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15OrnamentOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure Ornament-specific capabilities of the map
    
    All margin values are relative to the `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/MapView.html) `‘s safe area. To allow the safe area (and thereby ornaments) to track the presence of navigation bars and tab bars, make MapView the root view of a view controller.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Structs/OrnamentOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentPosition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps16OrnamentPositionO) `
    
    Options used to configure the corner position of an ornament
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Enums/OrnamentPosition.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentVisibility](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18OrnamentVisibilityO) `
    
    Options used to configure the visibility of an ornament
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Enums/OrnamentVisibility.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionButtonOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps24AttributionButtonOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure position, margin, and visibility for the map’s attribution button.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Structs/AttributionButtonOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[CompassViewOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18CompassViewOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure position, margin, image, and visibility for the map’s compass view.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Structs/CompassViewOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration

# Ornaments  Reference

[Ornaments\
=========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.4/Ornaments.html#/Ornaments)

*   `[OrnamentsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.4/Ornaments.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)OrnamentsManager) `

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.4/Classes/OrnamentsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.4/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15OrnamentOptionsV) `
    
    Used to configure Ornament-specific capabilities of the map
    
    All margin values are relative to the `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.4/Classes/MapView.html) `‘s safe area. To allow the safe area (and thereby ornaments) to track the presence of navigation bars and tab bars, make MapView the root view of a view controller.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.4/Structs/OrnamentOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OrnamentPosition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.4/Ornaments.html#/s:10MapboxMaps16OrnamentPositionO) `

# View Annotations  Reference

[View Annotations\
================](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/View%20Annotations.html#/View%20Annotations)

*   `[ViewAnnotationManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationManagerC) `
    
    Manager API to control View Annotations.
    
    View annotations are `UIView` instances that are drawn on top of the `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/Classes/MapView.html) ` and bound to some `[Geometry](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/GeoJSON.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8Geometrya) ` (only `Point` is supported for now). In case some view annotations intersect on the screen Z-index is based on addition order.
    
    View annotations are invariant to map camera transformations however such properties as size, visibility etc could be controlled by the user using update operation.
    
    View annotations are not explicitly bound to any sources however `[associatedFeatureId](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/Structs/ViewAnnotationOptions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationOptionsV19associatedFeatureIdSSSgvp) ` could be used to bind given view annotation with some `[Feature](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/GeoJSON.html#/s:10MapboxMaps7Featurea) ` by `Feature.identifier` meaning visibility of view annotation will be driven by visibility of given feature.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/Classes/ViewAnnotationManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewAnnotationUpdateObserver](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28ViewAnnotationUpdateObserverP) `
    
    An interface you use to detect when the map view lays out or updates visibility of annotation views.
    
    When visible portion of a map changes, e.g. responding to the user interaction, the map view adjusts the positions and visibility of its annotation views. Implement methods of `ViewAnnotationUpdateObserver` to detect when the map view updates position/size for supplied annotation views. As well as when annotation views get show/hidden when going in/out of visible portion of the map.
    
    To register an observer for view annotation updates, call the `[addViewAnnotationUpdateObserver(_:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/Classes/ViewAnnotationManager.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationManagerC03addcD14UpdateObserveryyAA0cdgH0_pF) ` method.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/Protocols/ViewAnnotationUpdateObserver.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewAnnotationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21ViewAnnotationOptionsV) `
    
    Stores layout and visibilty settings for a `ViewAnnotation`
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/Structs/ViewAnnotationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewAnnotationManagerError](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/View%20Annotations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps26ViewAnnotationManagerErrorO) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.2/Enums/ViewAnnotationManagerError.html)
    
    #### Declaration

---

*   `[FlyToCameraAnimator](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.15.0/Camera%20Animations.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)FlyToCameraAnimator) `
    
    An animator that evokes powered flight and an optional transition duration and timing function. It seamlessly incorporates zooming and panning to help the user find their bearings even after traversing a great distance.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.15.0/Classes/FlyToCameraAnimator.html)

# Expressions  Reference

[Expressions\
===========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Expressions.html#/Expressions)

*   `[Expression](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Expressions.html#/) `
    
    An `Expression` defines a formula for computing the value of any layout property, paint property, or filter within a map style. Expressions allow you to style data with multiple feature properties at once, apply conditional logic, and manipulate data with mathematical, logical, and string operators. This allows for sophisticated runtime styling.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Structs/Expression.html)
    
*   `[Exp](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Expressions.html#/) `
    
    An `[Expression](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Structs/Expression.html) ` defines a formula for computing the value of any layout property, paint property, or filter within a map style. Expressions allow you to style data with multiple feature properties at once, apply conditional logic, and manipulate data with mathematical, logical, and string operators. This allows for sophisticated runtime styling.
    
*   `[FormatOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Expressions.html#/) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Structs/FormatOptions.html)
    
*   `[NumberFormatOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Expressions.html#/) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Structs/NumberFormatOptions.html)
    
*   `[CollatorOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Expressions.html#/) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.2/Structs/CollatorOptions.html)

*   `[Exp](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps3Expa) `
    
    An `[Expression](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Structs/Expression.html) ` defines a formula for computing the value of any layout property, paint property, or filter within a map style. Expressions allow you to style data with multiple feature properties at once, apply conditional logic, and manipulate data with mathematical, logical, and string operators. This allows for sophisticated runtime styling.
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[FormatOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13FormatOptionsV) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Structs/FormatOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[NumberFormatOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps19NumberFormatOptionsV) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Structs/NumberFormatOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[CollatorOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15CollatorOptionsV) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.11.0/Structs/CollatorOptions.html)

# Other  Reference

[Other\
=====](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.0/Other.html#/Other)

*   `[Cancelable](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.0/Other.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10CancelableP) `
    
    A type that conforms to `Cancelable` typically represents a long running operation that can be canceled. Custom implementations must behave correctly if `[cancel()](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.0/Protocols/Cancelable.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10CancelableP6cancelyyF) ` is invoked more than once.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.18.0/Protocols/Cancelable.html)
    
    #### Declaration

---

# Utilities  Reference

[Utilities\
=========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.5/Utilities.html#/Utilities)

*   `[CompassDirectionFormatter](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.5/Utilities.html#/s:10MapboxMaps25CompassDirectionFormatterC) `
    
    The `CompassDirectionFormatter` class provides properly formatted descriptions of absolute compass directions. For example, a value of `90` may be formatted as “east”, depending on the locale.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.5/Classes/CompassDirectionFormatter.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Projection](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.5/Utilities.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10ProjectionC) `

     projection methods.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.5/Classes/Projection.html)
    
    #### Declaration

*   `[FlyToCameraAnimator](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.12.3/Camera%20Animations.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)FlyToCameraAnimator) `

[### ANCHOR](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/ANCHOR)

*   `[Anchor](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps6AnchorO) `
    
    Whether extruded geometries are lit relative to the map or viewport.

[### SYMBOL\_Z\_ORDER](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/SYMBOL_Z_ORDER)

*   `[SymbolZOrder](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps12SymbolZOrderO) `
    
    Determines whether overlapping symbols in the same layer are rendered in the order that they appear in the data source or by their y-position relative to the viewport. To control the order and prioritization of symbols otherwise, use `symbol-sort-key`.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Enums/SymbolZOrder.html)
    
    #### Declaration

    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_ROTATION\_ALIGNMENT](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_ROTATION_ALIGNMENT)

*   `[TextRotationAlignment](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21TextRotationAlignmentO) `
    
    In combination with `symbol-placement`, determines the rotation behavior of the individual glyphs forming the text.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Enums/TextRotationAlignment.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_TRANSFORM](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_TRANSFORM)

*   `[TextTransform](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13TextTransformO) `
    
    Specifies how to capitalize text, similar to the CSS `text-transform` property.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Enums/TextTransform.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_TRANSLATE\_ANCHOR](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_TRANSLATE_ANCHOR)

*   `[TextTranslateAnchor](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps19TextTranslateAnchorO) `
    
    Controls the frame of reference for `text-translate`.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Enums/TextTranslateAnchor.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_WRITING\_MODE](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_WRITING_MODE)

*   `[TextWritingMode](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15TextWritingModeO) `
    
    The property allows control over a symbol’s orientation. Note that the property values act as a hint, so that a symbol whose language doesn’t support the provided orientation will be laid out in its natural orientation. Example: English point symbol will be rendered horizontally even if array value contains single ‘vertical’ enum value. The order of elements in an array define priority order for the placement of an orientation variant.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Enums/TextWritingMode.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Visibility](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10VisibilityO) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.6.0/Enums/Visibility.html)
    
    #### Declaration

# Expressions  Reference

[Expressions\
===========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Expressions.html#/Expressions)

*   `[Expression](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10ExpressionV) `
    
    An `Expression` defines a formula for computing the value of any layout property, paint property, or filter within a map style. Expressions allow you to style data with multiple feature properties at once, apply conditional logic, and manipulate data with mathematical, logical, and string operators. This allows for sophisticated runtime styling.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Structs/Expression.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Exp](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps3Expa) `
    
    An `[Expression](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Structs/Expression.html) ` defines a formula for computing the value of any layout property, paint property, or filter within a map style. Expressions allow you to style data with multiple feature properties at once, apply conditional logic, and manipulate data with mathematical, logical, and string operators. This allows for sophisticated runtime styling.
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[FormatOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13FormatOptionsV) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Structs/FormatOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[NumberFormatOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps19NumberFormatOptionsV) `
    
    Undocumented

    #### Declaration
    
*   `[CollatorOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Expressions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15CollatorOptionsV) `

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.10.0/Structs/CollatorOptions.html)

# Style  Reference

[Style\
=====](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/Style)

*   `[Style](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps5StyleC) `
    
    Style provides access to the APIs used to dynamically modify the map’s style. Use it to read and write layers, sources, and images. Obtain the Style instance for a MapView via MapView.mapboxMap.style.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Classes/Style.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[StyleURI](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8StyleURIV) `
    
    Enum representing the latest version of the Mapbox styles (as of publication). In addition, you can provide a custom URL or earlier version of a Mapbox style by using the `.custom` case.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Structs/StyleURI.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[LayerPosition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13LayerPositionO) `
    
    Specifies the position at which a layer will be added when using `Style.addLayer`.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Enums/LayerPosition.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[TransitionOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMTransitionOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Extensions/TransitionOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Light](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps5LightV) `
    
    The global light source.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Structs/Light.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Terrain](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps7TerrainV) `
    
    The global terrain source.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Structs/Terrain.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[StyleDecodable](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14StyleDecodableP) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Protocols/StyleDecodable.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[StyleEncodable](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Style.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14StyleEncodableP) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.1/Protocols/StyleEncodable.html)

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Structs/HeatmapLayer.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[HillshadeLayer](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Layers.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14HillshadeLayerV) `
    
    Client-side hillshading visualization based on DEM data. Currently, the implementation only supports Mapbox Terrain RGB and Mapzen Terrarium tiles.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Structs/HillshadeLayer.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[LineLayer](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Layers.html#/s:10MapboxMaps9LineLayerV) `
    
    A stroked line.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Structs/LineLayer.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[LocationIndicatorLayer](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Layers.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22LocationIndicatorLayerV) `
    
    Location Indicator layer.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Structs/LocationIndicatorLayer.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[RasterLayer](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Layers.html#/s:10MapboxMaps11RasterLayerV) `

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Structs/RasterLayer.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[SkyLayer](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Layers.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8SkyLayerV) `
    
    A spherical dome around the map that is always rendered behind all other layers.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Structs/SkyLayer.html)

*   `[SymbolLayer](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.8.2/Layers.html#/s:10MapboxMaps11SymbolLayerV) `

# Viewport  Reference

[Viewport\
========](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/Viewport)

*   `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC) `
    
    `Viewport` provides a structured approach to organizing camera management logic into states and transitions between them.
    
    At any given time, the viewport is either:
    
    *   idle (not updating the camera)
    *   in a state (camera is being managed by a `[ViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html) `)
    *   transitioning (camera is being managed by a `[ViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportTransition.html) `)
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewportOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ViewportOptionsV) `
    
    Configuraton options for `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/ViewportOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewportStatus](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14ViewportStatusO) `
    
    `ViewportStatus` contains 3 cases that express what the `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) ` is doing at any given time.
    
    The `[state(_:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Enums/ViewportStatus.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14ViewportStatusO5stateyAcA0C5State_pcACmF) ` and `[transition(_:toState:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Enums/ViewportStatus.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14ViewportStatusO10transitionyAcA0C10Transition_p_AA0C5State_ptcACmF) ` cases have associated values that are reference types, so equality and hash are implemented in terms of the identities of those objects.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Enums/ViewportStatus.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewportStatusObserver](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ViewportStatusObserverP) `
    
    `ViewportStatusObserver` must be implemented by objects that wish to register themselves using `[addStatusObserver(_:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC17addStatusObserveryyAA0ceF0_pF) ` so that they can observe `[status](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC6statusAA0C6StatusOvp) ` changes.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportStatusObserver.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewportStatusChangeReason](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps26ViewportStatusChangeReasonV) `
    
    Constants that describe why `[status](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC6statusAA0C6StatusOvp) ` changed.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/ViewportStatusChangeReason.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13ViewportStateP) `
    
    `ViewportState` is a protocol that `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) ` depends on as it orchestrates transitions to and from different states.
    
    A `ViewportState` is a reference type and must not be shared among multiple `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) ` instances simultaneously.
    
    The `[observeDataSource(with:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13ViewportStateP17observeDataSource4withAA10Cancelable_pSbAA13CameraOptionsVc_tF) ` method allows `[ViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportTransition.html) `s to consume a stream of camera updates from a target state while executing a transition. `[startUpdatingCamera()](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13ViewportStateP19startUpdatingCamerayyF) ` and `[stopUpdatingCamera()](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13ViewportStateP18stopUpdatingCamerayyF) ` are invoked to tell the state that it should assume or relinquish control of the map’s camera. These are typically used by `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) ` itself after a successful transition into a state and when exiting a state, respectively.
    
    MapboxMaps provides implementations of `ViewportState` that can be created and configured via methods on `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) `. Applications may also define their own implementations to handle advanced use cases not covered by the provided implementations.
    
    States should generally pre-warm their data sources as soon as they are created to minimize delays when they become current. For this reason, only states that are currently (or soon-to-be) needed should be kept alive so that unneeded resources (e.g. location services) can be released.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[FollowPuckViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps23FollowPuckViewportStateC) `
    
    A `[ViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html) ` implementation that tracks the location puck (to show a puck, use `[puckType](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/LocationOptions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15LocationOptionsV8puckTypeAA04PuckF0OSgvp) `)
    
    Use `[makeFollowPuckViewportState(options:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC014makeFollowPuckC5State7optionsAA0efcG0CAA0efcG7OptionsV_tF) ` to create instances of this class.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/FollowPuckViewportState.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[FollowPuckViewportStateOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps30FollowPuckViewportStateOptionsV) `
    
    Configuration options for `[FollowPuckViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/FollowPuckViewportState.html) `.
    
    Each of the `[CameraOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/CameraOptions.html) `\-related properties is optional, so that the state can be configured to only modify certain aspects of the camera if desired. This can be used, to achieve effects like allowing zoom gestures to work simultaneously with `[FollowPuckViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/FollowPuckViewportState.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/FollowPuckViewportStateOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[FollowPuckViewportStateBearing](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps30FollowPuckViewportStateBearingO) `
    
    Expresses the different ways that `[FollowPuckViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/FollowPuckViewportState.html) ` can obtain values to use when setting `[bearing](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/CameraOptions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13CameraOptionsV7bearingSdSgvp) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Enums/FollowPuckViewportStateBearing.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OverviewViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21OverviewViewportStateC) `
    
    A `[ViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html) ` implementation that shows an overview of the geometry specified by its `[geometry](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/OverviewViewportStateOptions.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28OverviewViewportStateOptionsV8geometry4Turf8GeometryOvp) `.
    
    Use `[makeOverviewViewportState(options:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC012makeOverviewC5State7optionsAA0ecF0CAA0ecF7OptionsV_tF) ` to create instances of this class.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/OverviewViewportState.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[OverviewViewportStateOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28OverviewViewportStateOptionsV) `
    
    Configuration options for `[OverviewViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/OverviewViewportState.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/OverviewViewportStateOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18ViewportTransitionP) `
    
    `ViewportTransition` is a protocol that `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) ` depends on as it orchestrates transitions to and from different `[ViewportState](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportState.html) `s.
    
    MapboxMaps provides implementations of `ViewportTransition` that can be created and configured via methods on `[Viewport](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html) `. Applications may also define their own implementations to handle advanced use cases not covered by the provided implementations.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportTransition.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[DefaultViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps25DefaultViewportTransitionC) `
    
    A default `[ViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportTransition.html) ` implementation.
    
    Use `[makeDefaultViewportTransition(options:)](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC011makeDefaultC10Transition7optionsAA0ecF0CAA0ecF7OptionsV_tF) ` to create instances of this class.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/DefaultViewportTransition.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[DefaultViewportTransitionOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps32DefaultViewportTransitionOptionsV) `
    
    Configuration options for `[DefaultViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/DefaultViewportTransition.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Structs/DefaultViewportTransitionOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ImmediateViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps27ImmediateViewportTransitionC) `
    
    A `[ViewportTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Protocols/ViewportTransition.html) ` implementation that transitions immediately without any animation.
    
    Use `[makeImmediateViewportTransition()](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.16.3/Classes/Viewport.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8ViewportC013makeImmediateC10TransitionAA0ecF0CyF) ` to create instances of this class.
    
    #### Declaration

*   `[PromoteId](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.7.0/Sources.html#/s:10MapboxMaps9PromoteIdO) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.7.0/Enums/PromoteId.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Scheme](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.7.0/Sources.html#/s:10MapboxMaps6SchemeO) `
    
    Influences the y direction of the tile coordinates. The global-mercator (aka Spherical Mercator) profile is assumed.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.7.0/Enums/Scheme.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Encoding](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.7.0/Sources.html#/s:10MapboxMaps8EncodingO) `
    
    The encoding used by this source. Mapbox Terrain RGB is used by default
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.7.0/Enums/Encoding.html)
    
    #### Declaration

[Camera Animations\

    APIs for animating the camera.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.14.0/Classes/CameraAnimationsManager.html)

*   `[CameraAnimator](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.14.0/Camera%20Animations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps14CameraAnimatorP) `

*   `[BasicCameraAnimator](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.14.0/Camera%20Animations.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)BasicCameraAnimator) `
    
    Undocumented

*   `[CameraTransition](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.14.0/Camera%20Animations.html#/s:10MapboxMaps16CameraTransitionV) `
    
    Structure used to represent a desired change to the map’s camera
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.14.0/Structs/CameraTransition.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[FlyToCameraAnimator](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.14.0/Camera%20Animations.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)FlyToCameraAnimator) `
    
    An animator that evokes powered flight and an optional transition duration and timing function. It seamlessly incorporates zooming and panning to help the user find their bearings even after traversing a great distance.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.14.0/Classes/FlyToCameraAnimator.html)

    The transition property for a layer. A transition property controls timing for the interpolation between a transitionable style property’s previous value and new value.

    Label placement relative to its geometry.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/SymbolPlacement.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### SYMBOL\_Z\_ORDER](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/SYMBOL_Z_ORDER)

*   `[SymbolZOrder](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps12SymbolZOrderO) `
    
    Determines whether overlapping symbols in the same layer are rendered in the order that they appear in the data source or by their y-position relative to the viewport. To control the order and prioritization of symbols otherwise, use `symbol-sort-key`.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/SymbolZOrder.html)
    
    #### Declaration

    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/TextAnchor.html)
    
    #### Declaration

*   `[TextPitchAlignment](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps18TextPitchAlignmentO) `
    
    Orientation of text when map is pitched.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/TextPitchAlignment.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_ROTATION\_ALIGNMENT](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_ROTATION_ALIGNMENT)

*   `[TextRotationAlignment](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps21TextRotationAlignmentO) `
    
    In combination with `symbol-placement`, determines the rotation behavior of the individual glyphs forming the text.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/TextRotationAlignment.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_TRANSFORM](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_TRANSFORM)

*   `[TextTransform](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps13TextTransformO) `
    
    Specifies how to capitalize text, similar to the CSS `text-transform` property.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/TextTransform.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_TRANSLATE\_ANCHOR](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_TRANSLATE_ANCHOR)

*   `[TextTranslateAnchor](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps19TextTranslateAnchorO) `
    
    Controls the frame of reference for `text-translate`.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/TextTranslateAnchor.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### TEXT\_WRITING\_MODE](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/TEXT_WRITING_MODE)

*   `[TextWritingMode](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15TextWritingModeO) `
    
    The property allows control over a symbol’s orientation. Note that the property values act as a hint, so that a symbol whose language doesn’t support the provided orientation will be laid out in its natural orientation. Example: English point symbol will be rendered horizontally even if array value contains single ‘vertical’ enum value. For symbol with point placement, the order of elements in an array define priority order for the placement of an orientation variant. For symbol with line placement, the default text writing mode is either \[‘horizontal’, ‘vertical’\] or \[‘vertical’, ‘horizontal’\], the order doesn’t affect the placement.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/TextWritingMode.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[Visibility](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Layer%20Property%20Values.html#/s:10MapboxMaps10VisibilityO) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.9.1/Enums/Visibility.html)
    
    #### Declaration

# MapView  Reference

[MapView\
=======](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/MapView)

*   `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapView) `
    
    Undocumented
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/MapView.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(cs)MapInitOptions) `
    
    Options used when initializing `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    Contains the `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `, `MapOptions` (including `GlyphsRasterizationOptions`) that are required to initialize a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/MapView.html) `.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/MapInitOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapInitOptionsProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/c:@M@MapboxMaps@objc(pl)MapInitOptionsProvider) `
    
    A protocol used to provide `[MapInitOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/MapInitOptions.html) ` when initializing a `[MapView](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/MapView.html) ` with a Storyboard or a nib.
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Protocols/MapInitOptionsProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    

[### ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/ResourceOptions)

*   `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps15ResourceOptionsV) `
    
    Options to configure access to a resource
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Structs/ResourceOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[ResourceOptionsManager](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps22ResourceOptionsManagerC) `
    
    Convenience class that manages a global `[ResourceOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Structs/ResourceOptions.html) `
    
    It’s possible to create `ResourceOptionsManager` instances as you need them, however it’s convenient to use the default object (`default`).
    
    For example, we recommend that the Mapbox access token be set in `application(_:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:)` rather than relying on the value in your application’s Info.plist:
    
        ```
        func application(_ application: UIApplication, didFinishLaunchingWithOptions launchOptions: [UIApplication.LaunchOptionsKey: Any]?) -> Bool {
            // Override point for customization after application launch.
            ResourceOptionsManager.default.resourceOptions.accessToken = "overridden-access-token"
            return true
        }
        ```
        
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Classes/ResourceOptionsManager.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[MapOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMMapOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Extensions/MapOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[GlyphsRasterizationOptions](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/c:objc(cs)MBMGlyphsRasterizationOptions) `
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Extensions/GlyphsRasterizationOptions.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[InterfaceOrientationProvider](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps28InterfaceOrientationProviderP) `
    
    A protocol that supplies current interface orientation for the specified view.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target (e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Protocols/InterfaceOrientationProvider.html)
    
    #### Declaration
    
*   `[AttributionURLOpener](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/MapView.html#/s:10MapboxMaps20AttributionURLOpenerP) `
    
    A protocol to open attribution URLs.
    
    Use this protocol when the map view is used in non-application target(e.g. application extension target).
    
    [See more](https://docs.mapbox.com/ios/maps/api/10.19.0/Protocols/AttributionURLOpener.html)
    
    #### Declaration

---
